title: TrustProfile
description: Cryptographic trust configuration for credential issuance and verification. Used by both issuance flows (which issuer keys are trusted) and verification flows (which credential issuers/roots are accepted). For org-specific framework overrides, see OrganizationTrustProfile.

profile_type.description: Top-level framework classification. Determines default trust anchors, certificate chain validation rules, and which PKD endpoints to consult.

check_mode.description: HARD_FAIL: reject credential if revocation check fails or is unavailable. SOFT_FAIL: accept with warning if check fails. SKIP: bypass revocation check entirely.

revocation_services.description: Revocation endpoint configuration for CRL, OCSP, and status list methods

auto_discover.description: Attempt to discover revocation endpoints from credential metadata

merge_discovered.description: Merge auto-discovered endpoints with explicitly configured ones

allowed_issuers.description: Explicit issuer allowlist. null = allow all not in denied_issuers.

denied_issuers.description: Explicit issuer denylist. Checked before allowed_issuers.

system_issuer_overrides.description: Per-issuer overrides for system issuers (e.g. ICAO-enrolled states). Keyed by issuer_id.

action.description: DENY: reject credentials from this issuer even though it's a trusted system issuer. DOWNGRADE: reduce trust_level for credentials from this issuer.

trust_level.description: Required when action is DOWNGRADE. Sets an effective trust level below the default 100.

revocation_profile_id.description: Optional reference to a RevocationProfile entity that governs revocation check mode, caching, and grace period. When set, takes precedence over inline revocation_policy configuration.

verification_policy_set_id.description: Reference to a PolicySet (policy_type=CREDENTIAL_VERIFICATION) containing Cedar policies for issuer trust decisions. When set, Cedar policies augment the allowed_issuers/denied_issuers lists: Cedar forbid policies act as an additional deny layer, and Cedar permit policies can grant access to issuers not in allowed_issuers.

compatible_compliance_codes.description: Compliance codes whose trust requirements this profile satisfies. Enables multiple compliance profiles (e.g., EUDI_PID and EUDI_MDL) to share a single EUDI trust registry. When null or absent, compatibility is inferred from profile_type alone.
